question: Which comment syntaxes can a Camunda 8.8 FEEL expression use to add inline explanation alongside the expression body?

options.text: Java-style `//` to end of line and `/* ... */` block comments.
Only Python-style `#` to end of line.
SQL-style `--` to end of line only.

a.text: The FEEL expressions introduction states that comments use Java-style syntax: `//` for the rest of a line and `/* ... */` for block comments, which can also span multiple lines.

b.text: `#` is not a FEEL comment marker; FEEL borrows Java-style comment syntax instead.

c.text: HTML/XML comment delimiters are not part of FEEL; only the documented Java-style forms are accepted.

d.text: SQL-style `--` is not recognised by the FEEL parser; the supported line comment is `//`.

explanation: FEEL supports Java-style comments: `//` introduces a comment that runs to the end of the line, and `/* ... */` introduces a block comment that may span multiple lines. Other styles such as `#`, `<!-- -->`, or `--` are not part of the FEEL grammar.
